title: (Sacramento) Today, Assemblymember David Alvarez (D-San Diego) issued the following statement on Assembly Bill 2560, which did not pass out of Senate Appropriations Committee:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The amendments made to AB 2560 would not serve the purpose of requiring coastal cities to build housing.",
    "Initial introduction of AB 2560 aimed to increase affordable housing opportunities for low and middle-income families on the coast.",
    "AB 2560 proposed to allow density bonus projects that build affordable housing to be implemented within the coastal zone of California.",
    "The housing proposed under AB 2560 could only be built on lots designated by local cities for housing like apartments and condos.",
    "AB 2560 limited housing development to areas where the environment and nature would not be impacted.",
    "Recent Senate amendments removed the original goal of AB 2560 by granting more authority to the Coastal Commission.",
    "The Coastal Commission has proven not to prioritize the building of housing.",
    "The requirement for cities to update their Local Coastal Programs with language that harmonizes density bonus law and the coastal act would fundamentally change how cities plan and build housing.",
    "The changes to AB 2560 would not make immediate nor significant improvements to the housing crisis."
  ]
}
```
